essence: David coordinates with a vendor on tonight's customer bookings and pricing (600-740 baht) and confirms she has been promoting the fruit juice/smoothie shop to her customers.
David plans to spin one task per brain (plus a new one) and run a 'brain order' process on each to organise workstreams systematically.

David studies kie.ai NanoBanana 2 (Gemini Flash-based, 14 reference images, ~6-9c per image, ComfyUI nodes) vs NanoBanana Pro and its Google-search-grounded infographic generation, then asks his assistant to refresh the image-model brain and track recent brain changes.

actions.text: Refresh kie-ai brain with NanoBanana 2 and web-search-grounded image generation for infographics
Identify which brains changed in the last three days

essence: Referencing Theodore/Samantha from the film 'Her', David muses to his agent about getting it involved in programming and vibe coding over the coming months while reviewing what to install.
With a brand-new machine in hand, David wants a system that provisions all his machines with a consistent software set — the seed of the later Ansible fleet-provisioning approach.

actions.text: Adopt a tool to provision new machines with a consistent software set

essence: David troubleshoots why closing a file in VS Code quits the whole app and asks how to shrink an 11MB file under a 10MB limit.

David directs his agent to SSH into the M4 Pro MacBook (via agentic-OS config or shell aliases), audit and copy his images directory to the current machine, fix its hostname — and sketches the idea of this evolving into a project-management tool that pushes tasks as notifications straight to Claude Code.

actions.text: Audit size/file count of /Users/davidcruwys/images on the M4 Pro and copy it to the current machine
Fix the MacBook hostname and run system updates

essence: David hunts for personal AppyDave brand photos of himself (PNGs, variously named) across his system and notes he should share the horizontal/agentic OS and personal-agent slide decks people have requested.

actions.text: Share horizontal OS, agentic OS, and personal-agent slide decks with requesters

essence: David settles the presentation structure: 10-18 slide architecture overview, then a live second-brain demo with the librarian agent extracting tag/frontmatter data, then vibe-coding a micro app with AppyStack to visualise it — and insists on being called AppyDave, not David Cruz.

actions.text: Build the session: architecture slides, second-brain + librarian demo, AppyStack micro-app generation from extracted data

essence: David flags automatic computer-to-computer delivery of Loom transcripts as one of the ideas he is integrating into the larger agentic system.

David iterates his positioning statement between the technical framing — building multi-agent teams across networked computers where human operators' agents train each other — and the simple framing 'I help people build micro apps'.

David corrects AI-generated session copy — he has been active since 1990 (not 'two decades') and is an educator of the BMAD method, not its creator — and requests five alternative session titles plus a goal-adjusted rewrite of the session description.

actions.text: Fix bio to 'since 1990' and 'educator of BMAD method, not creator' in the session copy and system text
Produce five alternative session titles and a goal-based rewrite of the session description

essence: David insists his machine-readable JSON brand and Dent documents (not just the Markdown handed to NotebookLM) should be surfaced, gathered into a temp folder, and packaged with a message asking a mate about converting them into infographics or diagrams.

actions.text: Collect brand + Dent JSON documents into a temp folder and open in Finder
Draft a message to a colleague about converting the JSON docs into infographics or diagrams

essence: David lays out his three-month blueprint 'Project Theodore' (after the film 'Her'): a five-computer agentic OS — M2 Mini, M4 Mini, a robot, plus Jan's and Mary's machines — replacing ad-hoc task requests with a systematic process built on shared process memory and a comprehension test for agents.

actions.text: Fill out the 'Agents in the World' document
